signals.headline: South Korea's annual inflation rose to 2
signals.raw:
South Korea's annual inflation rose to 2.2% in March 2026 | YES | HIGH confidence**

South Korea's annual inflation rate increased to 2.2% year-on-year in March 2026, up from 2.0% in January and February, though below market forecasts of 2.4%[2]. This follows steady 2.0% readings in prior months amid government efforts to stabilize food prices, with core inflation (excluding food and energy) at 2.
